_comment: Bit-flag registry for the `cards`, `actions`, and `magnetic_actions` flag columns. Cards is split into two host integers ŌĆö `cards.flags_state` (state flags, propagated forward by the bit-diff propagator in cards::write_at) and `cards.flags_bk` (bookkeeping flags + refcount holds, never bit-diff propagated). The two-field split replaces the implicit `BOOKKEEPING_FLAGS_MASK` separator with a structural one. All hold-style state is unified under refcount fields in `cards_bk` with a 'lock = never-released +1' idiom (see `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`). Single-bit flags use `bit` (a number); multi-bit fields use `bits` (an array of contiguous ascending positions, low-to-high). Append-only within each field ŌĆö once a bit (or set of bits) is assigned a name, never reuse it for a different meaning within the same field. Tombstones intentionally absent ŌĆö the schema cutover wiped data so there is no historical attachment to specific bit positions.

cards_state._comment: State flags ŌĆö what is true about the card. Propagated forward by `propagate_flag_diff_forward` in `cards::write_at` on every insert; the propagator diffs prev vs new and walks future-stamped rows extending the change until each bit hits a 'deliberate' value (where the row's bit doesn't match prev's). Honors `cards_bk.data_preserve` as a per-row opt-out. Hold-style state (slot_hold, position_locked, drop_locked, drop_hold) was retired from this field in the unified-hold-counts rework ŌĆö see `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`. All such concerns now live in `cards_bk` as refcount fields with the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom. Single bits 0..=4; multi-bit fields 5..=11 today, with 12..=31 free for growth.

dead.description: Server has marked this card as dead ŌĆö typically a recipe consumed it as a reagent. Set via UPDATE (rather than DELETE) so the write carries `valid_at`; the client treats the bit flip as the death event and times its dying animation against `valid_at` instead of receive time. Cards-only ŌĆö actions and magnetic_actions delete immediately and have no equivalent bit.

pos_need.description: Server *requires* this row to be at the written `micro_zone` / `micro_location` exactly. The client mirror must place this card at the asserted slot even if it conflicts with an already-loaded card; conflict resolution splices the incoming card INTO the target slot and re-anchors the prior occupant one step up the chain (the new card wins the slot). If the splice would push the chain past `MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH` the topmost card is evicted via the inventoryŌåÆlooseŌåÆnearby-tile cascade. Set on chain-stitch rows where the server has stitched an exact layout (`propose_action`); the client treats this as 'discard your local position guess, this is the truth.' Cleared at completion alongside the hold-count releases (no longer paired with a `slot_hold` bit ŌĆö the unified-counts rework moved that to `cards_bk.slot_hold_count`). Pair with [`pos_want`] for the *advisory* sibling.

magnetic.description: **Temporary** ŌĆö server has installed a magnetic action on this card. While set: the card is acting as the anchor of an in-flight magnetic recipe; the magnetic tick reducer or lifecycle resolver counts down toward success / failure. Set by `cards::write_at`'s install hook on the first row whose def declares a `lifecycle:` block, via definition-flag inheritance. Cleared by the magnetic tick reducer when the action terminates.

surface_locked.description: Card is locked to its current surface and may not be moved to a different surface. Kept as a single bit (not folded into a count) because there's no transient 'temporarily surface-locked' concept ŌĆö this is purely a static per-card property and the gate is binary.

is_owned_by_player.description: Disambiguates `Card.owner_id`. Set ŌåÆ `owner_id` is a `player_id` (only valid on soul cards ŌĆö the player who controls this avatar). Clear ŌåÆ `owner_id` is a `card_id` (the immediate container card; `0` reserved for 'world'). `cards::owning_player` walks `owner_id` upward, stopping at the first row carrying this bit and returning that row's `owner_id`; world-owned chains terminate at `owner_id == 0` and resolve to `WORLD_PLAYER_ID`. Server players `0..=1023` are reserved precisely so this walker has unambiguous targets at the soul boundary; namespace collisions between `player_id` and `card_id` numerics are resolved by THIS BIT, not by numeric range.

progress_style.description: 3-bit field encoding the progress bar style for this row's 'next future event' client render. The client reads `(flags_state >> 5) & 0b111` and renders accordingly. Values: 0 = no progress (no bar shown), 1 = ltr / cw default, 2 = rtl / ccw default. Values 3..=7 reserved for future styles. Set on the actor card's completion row by `action_completion::commit`; cleared on with-holds rows so non-event rows don't render bars. Multiple queued events on one card stack as a sequence of future rows each carrying their own style.

portrait_id.description: 4-bit portrait selector for soul cards. The client reads `(flags_state >> 8) & 0xF` and picks the matching portrait from the soul-def's portrait pack (e.g. `/textures/cards/soul/human/256_human_pack/256_human_<id+1>.png`). 16 portraits per soul def ŌĆö enough for a starter palette per race. Set once at soul spawn from a deterministic mix of `now_ms` / `player_id` / `card_id` and not mutated afterward; meaningless on non-soul cards.

pos_want.description: Server *prefers* this row to be at the written `micro_location` (and its `stack_state`/`stack_index`) but does not require it. The client mirror should place the card at the asserted slot when free; if the slot is occupied by an already-loaded card the incoming card STACKS ON TOP of the occupant (the existing card keeps its slot, the incoming card claims the next free index). If the resulting chain exceeds the index cap the topmost card is evicted via the inventoryŌåÆlooseŌåÆnearby-tile cascade. Pair with [`pos_need`] for the *required* sibling ŌĆö both share the splice primitive, differing only in which card wins the slot. Lifecycle mirrors `pos_need` (set at propose-time, cleared at completion).

zone_born.description: Card was generated from zone tile data (a promoted/materialized tile-card), as opposed to a player- or recipe-placed card. Set once at materialization (`find_or_create_tile_card`) and never flipped ŌĆö so it is safe in `cards_state` where the bit-diff propagator carries it forward harmlessly. Lets the client/server distinguish a bare terrain tile (zone_born, stackable-onto, re-derivable when synthetic) from a placed structure. Used by the snap/tile-resolution logic: a snapped root finds the zone_born card at its cell and stacks it as the `hex` branch member; a zone_born tile that loses its cell to a root pointer (when stacked) has its cell restored from server-side zone tracking. See `docs/micro_location_rewrite/00_design_and_plan.md`.

cards_bk._comment: Bookkeeping flags ŌĆö metadata about the row / write, plus refcounted hold fields. **Never** propagated by the bit-diff propagator (`flags_state` is its sole scope). The dirty / preserve markers are server-managed by `cards::write_at` based on the new-vs-prev row diff; callers should not set them manually except for the `*_preserve` opt-outs. The four hold-count fields (`position_hold_count`, `slot_share_count`, `drop_hold_count`, `slot_hold_count`) all use the same delta-arithmetic forward-propagator pattern (`propagate_<name>_forward`) and the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom ŌĆö permanent locks (formerly the `position_locked` / `drop_locked` bits in cards_state) are now expressed as a one-time increment at spawn that's never decremented. See `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`. The two `*_count` cap fields (`touch_count`, `server_count`) bound concurrent recipes / server-internal operations per card, preventing refcount overflow under realistic gameplay.

position_dirty.description: Server-managed marker ŌĆö auto-set by `cards::write_at` whenever the written row's `surface` / `macro_zone` / `micro_zone` / `micro_location` differ from the prior row's. Identifies rows whose *intent* was a position change. Used by `cards::scrub_or_repath_position_forward` (movement interruption) to recognize pure-position rows as droppable. Callers should not set this manually.

position_preserve.description: Caller-set marker ŌĆö this row's position fields must not be overridden by forward-propagation (movement scrub, teleport, etc.). Set by writes that pin a card's position as part of a recipe / chain stitch / magnetic pull, where movement should NOT yank the card mid-action. The scrub helper STOPS at the first row carrying this flag ŌĆö that row and everything past it are off-limits.

data_dirty.description: Server-managed marker ŌĆö auto-set by `cards::write_at` whenever the written row's `flags_state` / `owner_id` / `packed_definition` differ from the prior row's. Identifies rows whose *intent* was a non-position state change. Used by the position-scrub helper to distinguish pure-position rows (droppable) from mixed-or-data rows (keep, update position fields only). Callers should not set this manually.

data_preserve.description: Caller-set marker ŌĆö this row's `flags_state` / `owner_id` / `packed_definition` must not be overridden by forward-propagation (the per-bit forward-prop in `cards::write_at`). Set by writes that establish a future state deliberately, even when that state happens to equal the inherited value (which the per-bit stop-on-change rule can't distinguish from 'never touched'). The forward-prop SKIPS rows carrying this flag.

position_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count of how many systems currently claim a position hold on this card (chain stitch, magnetic pull, has-predicate match, etc.). Readers asking 'is this card position-held?' check `count > 0`. Saturates at `0b111 = 7`; in realistic play the count tops out around 2-3 so the cap is comfortable slack. Forward-propagated by `cards::propagate_position_hold_forward` with delta arithmetic (+1 on acquire, -1 on release applied to every future row), so overlapping holders compose correctly across future-stamped completion rows. **Absorbs `position_locked`** (formerly a single bit in `cards_state`) via the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom ŌĆö permanent locks (world tiles, anchored event cards) call `acquire_position_hold` once at spawn and never release. The combined gate 'is this card position-pinned?' becomes a single `count > 0` check.

slot_share_count.description: 3-bit reference count for shared (non-exclusive) holds ŌĆö `borrow.` / `share.` iterators in `propose_action` increment; their completion-row release in `action_completion::commit` decrements. Sibling to `slot_hold_count` (exclusive ŌĆö `claim.` / `use.`). `validate_bindings` rejects new `claim.` / `use.` iterators against this card if `count > 0` (can't exclusively claim a card someone is currently borrowing); new borrows succeed (further increment) as long as `slot_hold_count == 0`. Saturates at 7. Mirrors `position_hold_count`'s delta-arithmetic forward-prop pattern.

drop_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count gating 'cards may not be stacked onto this one as a child.' Permanent locks (formerly `cards_state.drop_locked`) call `acquire_drop_hold` once at spawn and never release ŌĆö the `+1` floor keeps `count > 0` forever. Transient server-side blocks (e.g., during a magnetic pull where dropping onto the anchor would disrupt the in-flight resolution) increment and decrement normally. The drop-onto-target gate fires in `chain_stitch`, `equip_card`'s target attachment, and any other server site that writes a child's `micro_location` to point at this card. **Note:** the client-side 'I'm currently dragging this card; don't accept other local drops onto it' concern was historically conflated with this field but the server has no concept of client drag operations ŌĆö that lives separately as `DragHoldStore` (client sidecar).

slot_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count for exclusive (`claim.` / `use.`) recipe holds. **Absorbs the former `slot_hold` single bit in `cards_state`** under the unified-counts rework. By design at most one normal claim coexists at a time (the validate-time gate `slot_hold_count > 0` rejects further claims), so the field is typically 0 or 1 in steady state. The extra 2 bits buy safety headroom against propagation bugs and let future server-internal paths layer a `+1` lock on top of an in-flight claim if needed. `validate_bindings` rejects both `claim` / `use` (always) and `borrow` / `share` (always) when `count > 0` ŌĆö preserves the claim/borrow asymmetry from the pre-rework matrix because `slot_share_count` remains a separate field for shared holds. Cleared by `action_completion::commit`'s release pass for transient claims; permanent server-internal locks (rare) would never release.

touch_count.description: 2-bit (max 3) reference count of in-flight client-initiated recipes touching this card. Incremented once per card per recipe by `apply_locks`; decremented once per card per recipe by `action_completion::commit`. `validate_bindings` rejects any client `propose_action` whose touched card has `touch_count >= 3` ŌĆö the cap prevents the underlying hold-count fields (slot_share_count, position_hold_count) from approaching their u3 saturation under realistic gameplay. Capped at 3 (not 4) because the 'free' value 4..=7 in a u3 hold count is reserved as overflow headroom for forward-prop edge cases. Server-internal paths bypass this gate and use `server_count` instead.

server_count.description: 2-bit (max 3) reference count of server-internal touches (magnetic install, GC slot_hold, scheduled-reducer paths, admin operations). Same shape as `touch_count` but on a separate field so server activity can never starve the client and vice versa. Server-initiated paths use this; client-recipe paths use `touch_count`. The combined max touches (3 + 3 = 6) leaves 1 slot of headroom on the u3 hold-count fields before saturation.

tile_stock_0.description: 2-bit stock slot 0 for promoted tile-cards (`card_type` in {tile, tile_decorator, event}). Mirrors the Zone tile slot's `stock0` field; seeded from the zone on `find_or_create_tile_card`, written by `action_completion`'s `ModifyTileStock` effect via `cards::set_tile_stock`, and folded back into the zone on GC demotion. Meaningless on non-tile cards (those leave the bits at 0). No delta-propagator ŌĆö stock writes are absolute and apply at the row's `valid_at`; the existing `prior_at` semantics give downstream readers the correct value. See `docs/TILE_AS_CARD.md`.

tile_stock_1.description: Sibling of `tile_stock_0` for slot 1. Same seeding / write / demotion / propagation discipline.

micro_is_card.description: Discriminates `micro_location`. Set ŌåÆ `micro_location` is a root card_id and this card is a stack member (branch = `stack_state`, slot = `stack_index`). Clear ŌåÆ `micro_location` is packed loose coords + offset `[local_q:3 | local_r:3 | x:12 | y:12 | rsvd:2]`. Lives in `flags_bk` (NOT `flags_state`) so it carries forward by row-cloning in lockstep with `micro_location` rather than being walked independently by the `flags_state` bit-diff propagator ŌĆö keeping the discriminator and the value it gates consistent across a card's row history. Always written together with `micro_location` via the `set_stacked` / `set_loose` helpers.

stack_state.description: 2-bit branch/kind, gated on `micro_is_card`. Set (stacked): 0=hex, 1=top, 2=bottom, 3=deferred (matches `STACK_DIR_HEX/UP/DOWN` + `STACK_STATE_DEFERRED` in packed.rs). Clear (loose): 0=loose-hex, 1=loose-rect, 2=snap-hex, 3=snap-rect (`LOOSE_*`/`SNAP_*`). In `flags_bk` for the same lockstep-with-`micro_location` reason as `micro_is_card`. Replaces the former 2-bit stacked-state that lived in the removed `micro_zone` byte.

stack_index.description: 4-bit slot index within a branch (0..15), used only when `micro_is_card` is set. Gives the card's position in its root's chain for the given `stack_state` branch. Gap-tolerant: a deleted member leaves a hole (no renumber); placement claims the next free index and fails over to loose on overflow. In `flags_bk` (lockstep with `micro_location`). Replaces the former `position` nibble in the removed `micro_zone` byte.
